Empires & Puzzles Costumed Toxicandra Review: The Floral Sovereign Returns

Toxicandra is back with a new look and a massive stat injection. With 750 Boosted Health at Fast speed, she might just be the most efficient support hero in the game. We break down why the 'Sovereign of Flora' is terrorizing the current meta.
Costumed Toxicandra Review: From Fragile Flower to Overhealing Powerhouse
Costumed Toxicandra: The Sovereign of Flora

Toxicandra has returned from the League of Villains, shedding her original "swampy" vibe for a majestic, floral regality. Released in mid-2025, her costume addresses every single complaint players had about her aging base form. Gone is the capped 30% heal; in its place is massive

. Gone are the paper-thin stats; in their place is a modern stat block that rivals the latest tower heroes. She is a Fast speed, Druid-class overhealer who cleanses and boosts mana—a combination that is frankly terrifying in the right hands.
The transformation is stunning. While the original Toxicandra looked like a poisonous plant come to life, the

is regal, commanding, and vibrant. The "Rose" motif suggests beauty with thorns, fitting for a hero who heals your team while inflicting Curse damage on the enemy. It’s a complete visual overhaul that signals her ascension from a niche support to a meta-defining powerhouse.
The costume brings Toxicandra roaring into the modern era. Her power sits near
before troops or limit breaks. This is a massive jump from her base form's ~1300 health.

Compared to late-2025 heroes (who hover around 1260 Power), she is extremely competitive. She isn't the absolute highest stat stick in the game, but she is tanky enough to survive sniper hits and has enough attack to make her tile damage relevant.
This skill is perfection for a support hero.
speed (7-8 tiles), this is incredible. Most boosted healers are Average or Slow. Popping 750 extra HP on your team every few turns makes your team incredibly difficult to kill.
Just like her base form, she wipes all ailments. With the prevalence of "Uncleansable" effects shifting back to cleansable "Stacks" or heavily punishing DoTs, a fast cleanse is top-tier.
She keeps the engine running. The moderate mana gain ensures that your slower heavy hitters charge up faster, creating a snowball effect.
Fast. With a Level 29 Mana troop or equivalent, she fires in 7 tiles.
750 HP flat. This is significantly better than 30% for any ally with less than 2500 Max HP (which is most of them).

Family Bonus, Passives, Aether Power & Unique Abilities
The costume breathes new life into her family synergy and passive kit.
The Villain family bonus is still niche, but with updated stats, the "counter-damage" or "healing chance" actually has impactful numbers behind it now.
This is a fantastic addition. Every time she heals your team, she softens up the enemy team with DoT. It breaks focus and can finish off low-HP enemies hiding behind minions.
gives her innate resistance to special skill damage. This is perfect for a healer, ensuring she doesn't get sniped before she can pop her boosted health.
Base Toxicandra was a legend. Costumed Toxicandra is a god. She takes the legacy of "Fast Support" and updates it for the "Overheal Meta."
. There are very few heroes who can match her speed-to-healing ratio. She is arguably better than
in offense because of the Cleanse (Hathor blocks, but Toxicandra fixes what's already broken) and the direct mana gain.
She is a staple for top 100 alliance wars and high-level raiding.

(essential), better stats, and the Druid class (Minion meat shield). The Base form's Cleric class is the only argument for it, but the stat drop-off is too severe.
It provides huge stat multipliers (Attack, Defense, Health, Mana). Even if you use the base look (why would you?), the bonus makes the hero viable.
As a Druid, she spawns minions which add to her survivability.
Maximizes the HP of her Thorn Minions (which scale off her HP). Makes her incredibly hard to kill.

High stats + Special Armor Aether Power + Druid Minions = Very Tanky.

If you get her, she is your main healer. Forever. Prioritize leveling her immediately.

She is worth the Alpha Aethers. The LB2 stat boost makes her minion beefier and her overheal harder to punch through. She replaces older green healers like Mother North or Alberich in non-Rush formats because she is proactive rather than reactive.
"A fast healer, who speeds herself up... She will be at least respected as much as C. Hathor if not more!"

- The consensus is clear: Boosted Health wins.

Costumed Toxicandra is a masterclass in how to revitalize an old favorite. She keeps the soul of the original (Fast, Cleanse, Mana) and injects it with the serum of modern power creep (Boosted Health, Stats). She is elegant, deadly, and arguably the best green healer in the game right now.
An absolute must-have. A solid S-Tier healer who will anchor your teams for years.
